page: Microsoft Azure M64s is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8280M processor, providing 64 dedicated vCPUs across 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz and includes a 38 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. It also includes 2097 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics are relatively weak, as shown by poor scores in single-threaded gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench single-core tests. However, multi-core and memory-intensive workloads perform significantly better. The server demonstrates strong memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks in both read and write operations, alongside strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and Redis SET operations fall within average ranges.

Given its massive memory allocation of 1 TB RAM and dedicated compute resources, the M64s offers high resource density for memory-bound applications, though single-core performance tradeoffs should be considered. It is highly suitable for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems, multi-threaded data processing, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity and local storage without the need for GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure M64s is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 1024.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8280M processor with x86_64 architecture. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server provides 32 physical cores with hyper-threading and a 38 MB L3 cache, alongside 2097 GB of local storage. Performance benchmarks reveal weak single-core execution, but strong multi-core capabilities and high memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks. The system supports Premium Storage and delivers average performance in database operations and Redis SET operations, balancing high memory capacity with reliable multi-threaded throughput. This hardware profile makes the M64s qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads rather than raw single-threaded compute. It is best suited for large-scale databases, multi-threaded data processing, and in-memory caching applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M64s is a memory-optimized virtual machine with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 2097 GB local storage, delivering strong memory bandwidth for database and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M64s is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1024 GB of RAM, and 2097 GB of local storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring one terabyte of RAM and sixty-four dedicated vCPUs for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8280M processor
1024.0 GB of system memory providing a high 16.0 GB per core ratio
2097 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong memory bandwidth performance for large uncached read and write operations
Optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and multi-threaded data processing
